---
title: "Authoring Feature Views"
subtitle: "SQL and dbplyr patterns from simple aggregates to wide and temporal features"
---

## Overview

This chapter covers **defining features on the warehouse** and registering them as Feature Views from R — the step *before* materializing training data or reading into R.

The snowflakeR Feature Store trilogy in this guide:

| Chapter | Question |
|---------|----------|
| [Feature Store](../17_feature_store/index.qmd) | What is the store, and how do I register entities, join spines, and generate training? |
| **Authoring Feature Views** (this chapter) | How do I write the SQL or dbplyr that *produces* features? |
| [Training Data in R](../17a_training_data_in_r/index.qmd) | After `save_as` / export, how do I get OBJECT/ARRAY/VECTOR into R efficiently? |

::: {.callout-note}
## Draft outline

This chapter is a **scoped outline** — section structure and cross-links are stable; worked SQL/dbplyr examples will be added incrementally. Until then, use [Feature Store — Feature views](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-views), the [`feature-store` vignette](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflakeR/articles/feature-store.html), and the [Feature Store Implementation Guide](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflake-featurestore-imp-guide/) for full examples.
:::

Platform background on hybrid OBJECT payloads, compile cost, and sparse arrays: [Implementation Guide — Advanced patterns §12.10](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflake-featurestore-imp-guide/12_advanced_patterns/index.html#sec-wide-sparse).

## Learning Objectives

When this chapter is complete, you will be able to:

- Choose managed vs external Feature Views and draft-then-register vs one-step creation
- Author entity-level aggregates in SQL and dbplyr as `feature_df`
- Add event-level features with `timestamp_col` for point-in-time joins
- Build rolling / tile aggregates with `sfr_feature()` and `feature_granularity`
- Compose multi-step feature SQL with dbplyr `compute()` ([RSnowflake Query](../13_rsnowflake_query/index.qmd#sec-etl-temp))
- Register OBJECT and ARRAY bundles for wide feature sets ([RSnowflake Query — dbplyr](../13_rsnowflake_query/index.qmd#sec-snowflake-dbplyr))
- Know when to defer to the [Implementation Guide — Temporal features](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflake-featurestore-imp-guide/06_temporal_features/index.html) for advanced patterns

## Prerequisites

- [Feature Store — Setup](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-setup) and at least one [entity](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-entities)
- RSnowflake + dbplyr for lazy `feature_df` pipelines ([RSnowflake: Query](../13_rsnowflake_query/index.qmd))
- Optional: [`workspace_feature_store.ipynb`](https://github.com/Snowflake-Labs/snowflakeR/blob/main/inst/notebooks/workspace_feature_store.ipynb) for a runnable Workspace walkthrough

---

## Progression: simple → complex {#sec-progression}

| Stage | Feature shape | Registration hook | Planned section |
|-------|---------------|-------------------|-----------------|
| 1 | One row per entity; scalar columns | SQL or dbplyr `summarise()` | [§Entity aggregates](#sec-entity-aggregates) |
| 2 | You maintain the table (dbt/ETL) | External view, no `refresh_freq` | [§External views](#sec-external-views) |
| 3 | One row per entity per event time | `timestamp_col` | [§Event-level features](#sec-event-level) |
| 4 | Rolling sums / counts over time windows | `sfr_feature()`, `feature_granularity` | [§Tile and window aggregates](#sec-tile-windows) |
| 5 | Multi-step rollups, joins, windows | dbplyr + `compute()` temp tables | [§Multi-step pipelines](#sec-multi-step) |
| 6 | Hundreds of features | OBJECT / ARRAY / typed packs | [§Wide and sparse bundles](#sec-wide-bundles) |

After registration, continue with [Feature Store — Generate training data](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-training) and [Training Data in R](../17a_training_data_in_r/index.qmd).

---

## Entity-level aggregates {#sec-entity-aggregates}

**Goal:** One feature row per entity (e.g. `customer_id`) — `AVG(order_total)`, `COUNT(*)`, `MAX(order_date)`.

**Planned content:**

- SQL `feature_df` string with `GROUP BY` entity key
- Equivalent dbplyr: `tbl()` → `group_by()` → `summarise()` → `sfr_create_feature_view(..., feature_df = lazy_tbl)`
- Managed view with `refresh_freq` vs one-step `sfr_create_feature_view()`
- Draft workflow: `sfr_feature_view()` → inspect → `sfr_register_feature_view()`

**Existing examples today:** [Feature Store — Managed views](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-views), [Feature Store — dbplyr as feature_df](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-views), vignette [`feature-store`](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflakeR/articles/feature-store.html).

---

## External views {#sec-external-views}

**Goal:** Register metadata over a table your pipeline already maintains — no Dynamic Table refresh from Feature Store.

**Planned content:**

- Omit `refresh_freq`; point `feature_df` at dbt/ETL output
- Timestamp column on external tables for point-in-time correctness
- When external + managed views compose in one training join

**Existing examples today:** [Feature Store — External views](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-views).

---

## Event-level features {#sec-event-level}

**Goal:** Features that change over time — activity logs, session metrics — with correct ASOF joins at training time.

**Planned content:**

- `timestamp_col` on the Feature View
- Spine `spine_timestamp_col` alignment
- Contrast with entity-level aggregates (latest value vs history)

**Existing examples today:** [Feature Store — Time-series features](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-views), [Feature Store — Point-in-time join](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-pit).

**Platform depth:** [Implementation Guide — Temporal features](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflake-featurestore-imp-guide/06_temporal_features/index.html).

---

## Tile and window aggregates {#sec-tile-windows}

**Goal:** Rolling-window features (e.g. sum of amount over 1 hour / 1 day) without hand-writing window SQL for every feature.

**Planned content:**

- `sfr_feature(name, dtype, agg_func, window)` list
- `feature_granularity` and event-level source `feature_df`
- SQL equivalent patterns with `SUM(...) OVER (...)` for readers who prefer explicit windows ([RSnowflake Query — Joins and window functions](../13_rsnowflake_query/index.qmd#sec-windows))

**Existing examples today:** vignette [`feature-store` — Tile-based aggregation](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflakeR/articles/feature-store.html) (not yet expanded in this guide chapter).

---

## Multi-step feature pipelines {#sec-multi-step}

**Goal:** Break complex feature logic into named warehouse steps instead of one nested query or R-side `collect()`.

**Planned content:**

- Step 1: daily rollups → `compute(temporary = TRUE)`
- Step 2: 90-day aggregates from temp table
- Register the **final** lazy table or view SQL as `feature_df`
- `show_query()` before registration

**Existing examples today:** [RSnowflake Query — Multi-step ETL](../13_rsnowflake_query/index.qmd#sec-etl-temp) (generic dbplyr; Feature Store registration glue to be added here).

---

## Wide and sparse bundles {#sec-wide-bundles}

**Goal:** Avoid thousand-column Feature Views — pack features into OBJECT or typed ARRAY columns.

**Planned content:**

- `OBJECT_CONSTRUCT` / `object_agg()` in SQL and dbplyr
- Typed `ARRAY` packs and embeddings
- Hybrid key columns + OBJECT payload (platform pattern)
- Link forward to training export: [Training Data in R — Pipelines B–D](../17a_training_data_in_r/index.qmd#sec-e2e-pipelines)

**Existing examples today:** [Feature Store — Wide and sparse](../17_feature_store/index.qmd#sec-wide-sparse), [RSnowflake Query — Semi-structured dbplyr](../13_rsnowflake_query/index.qmd#sec-snowflake-dbplyr), [Implementation Guide §12.10](https://snowflake-labs.github.io/snowflake-featurestore-imp-guide/12_advanced_patterns/index.html#sec-wide-sparse).

---

## Validate before register {#sec-validate}

**Goal:** Catch schema and key issues before materializing a Dynamic Table.

**Planned content:**

- `head()` / `count()` on lazy `feature_df` via RSnowflake
- Entity key uniqueness checks in SQL
- Column naming for `auto_prefix` multi-view joins
- `sfr_describe_feature_view()` after registration

---

## Related chapters {#sec-related}

- [Feature Store](../17_feature_store/index.qmd) — entities, training generation, inference, online serving  
- [Training Data in R](../17a_training_data_in_r/index.qmd) — read materialized training into R (ADBC, VECTOR, pipelines)  
- [RSnowflake: Query](../13_rsnowflake_query/index.qmd) — dbplyr push-down, windows, `compute()`  
- [Model Registry](../18_model_registry/index.qmd) — lineage from `sfr_generate_dataset()`
